reasoning: Lilly King set the 100m breaststroke long course world record at 1:04.13 in 2017, and it has stood for 8 years despite challenges from elite swimmers. The forecast period spans late October 2025 to late January 2026. In the international swimming calendar, this time of year is primarily devoted to short-course (25m) or short-course yards competitions (such as the World Cup, domestic short course championships, and American collegiate swimming). There are very few major long course meters (LCM) competitions during these months where athletes would be fully tapered to break a longstanding world record. Even when regional LCM meets like the US Open or certain national championships happen in December, they rarely produce world records. Because no major international LCM games (Olympics, World Championships, Asian Games, Commonwealth Games) are scheduled between November 2025 and January 2026, the likelihood of this world record being broken is exceptionally low. Therefore, the probability that Lilly King still holds the record on these dates is very high, decreasing just slightly over time to account for the longer window of risk.
